NAME: SINGER
FULL NAME: sampling and inferring of genealogies with recombination
KEYWORDS: Ancestral Recombination Graph (ARG), Bayesian inference, population genomics, genealogical analysis, archaic introgression, trans-species polymorphism
DESCRIPTION: SINGER is a Bayesian method for accelerating ARG (Ancestral Recombination Graph) sampling from the posterior distribution, enabling accurate inference and uncertainty quantification for hundreds of whole-genome sequences. It addresses scalability and accuracy challenges in ARG reconstruction, improving robustness to model misspecification. Applications include detecting population differentiation, archaic introgression, and trans-species polymorphism in regions like the HLA locus.
MANUAL_CITATION: Deng, Y., Nielsen, R., & Song, Y.S. Robust and accurate Bayesian inference of genome-wide genealogies for hundreds of genomes. Nature Genetics, 57, 2124–2135. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41588-025-02317-9
ARROW_SUMMARY: Phased WGS → Bayesian MCMC sampling (threading, ARG re-scaling, SGPR moves) → Genome-wide ARGs with uncertainty quantification
DATA_REQUIRED: Phased whole-genome sequences. Requires phasing; not suitable for unphased or ancient DNA without prior phasing.
CITATION: Deng, Y., Nielsen, R., & Song, Y.S. Robust and accurate Bayesian inference of genome-wide genealogies for hundreds of genomes. Nature Genetics, 57, 2124–2135. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41588-025-02317-9
